title: Creamy Sweet Potato With Ginger Soup (Community Enhanced)
ingredients: 1.5 pounds canned yams (or 1 large sweet potato + 3 white potatoes)
1 large onion, cut into large dice
1.5 teaspoons fresh grated ginger (or ground ginger)
3-4 cups chicken broth, homemade or from a carton or can
1.5 cups heavy cream (or half-and-half, whole milk, or 2% milk)
Salt and lots of freshly ground black pepper, to taste
Optional: roasted Brussels sprouts and peppers
instructions: If using Brussels sprouts and peppers, roast them separately until golden and set aside.

Heat oil over medium-high heat in a large, deep saute pan until shimmering. Add sweet potatoes, then onion; saute, stirring very little at first, then more frequently, until vegetables start to turn golden brown, 7 to 8 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and add butter, sugar and garlic; continue cooking until all vegetables are a rich spotty caramel color, about 10 minutes longer.
Add ginger, nutmeg and extra cayenne pepper; continue to saute until fragrant, 30 seconds to 1 minute longer.
Add 3 cups broth initially; bring to a simmer over medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer, partially covered, until sweet potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes.

Using an immersion blender or traditional blender, puree until very smooth, 30 seconds to 1 minute. (If using a traditional blender, vent it either by removing the lid's pop-out center or by lifting one edge of the lid. Drape the blender canister with a kitchen towel. To 'clean' the canister, pour in a little cream, blend briefly, then add to the soup.)
Return to pan (or a soup pot); add enough cream/milk so the mixture is souplike. If too thick, add more broth. Add lots of black pepper. Taste, and add salt if needed.
Heat through, ladle into bowls. If using, add roasted vegetables on top and drizzle with heavy cream. Garnish with peanuts and serve.

source_review.text: I made this with canned yams. I added roasted Brussels Sprouts and peppers. I drizzled heavy cream at the end. Loved the contrast of the roasted greens to the sweet and spicy. Turned out amazing!

modifications_applied.reasoning: Canned yams provide convenience, roasted vegetables add texture contrast, heavy cream adds richness.

changes_made.from_text: 1.5 pounds peeled raw sweet potatoes, cut into 1-inch chunks

changes_made.to_text: Optional: roasted Brussels sprouts and peppers
drizzle with heavy cream at the end

source_review.text: It was amazing!! The portion is only enough for 2-3 people. Very good as is but when I make it again I will use fresh ginger to give it more of a ginger flavour.

modifications_applied.reasoning: Fresh ginger provides more intense, vibrant ginger flavor compared to ground ginger.

changes_made.to_text: 1.5 teaspoons fresh grated ginger (or ground ginger)

source_review.text: I had one large sweet potato that needed to be used, and 3 white potatoes, so I used those to make this soup. I also substituted 2% milk instead of half-and-half or whole milk. It turned out delicious! I highly recommend it. Thanks for sharing the recipe.

modifications_applied.reasoning: Mixed potatoes provide different textures and flavors. Lower fat milk options accommodate dietary preferences.

changes_made.to_text: 1.5 pounds canned yams (or 1 large sweet potato + 3 white potatoes)
1.5 cups heavy cream (or half-and-half, whole milk, or 2% milk)

source_review.text: Def has a kick! Very thick.. Even w 2% milk- will use more broth next time. Used fresh grated ginger. Hearty and yummy!

modifications_applied.reasoning: Adding more broth prevents the soup from being too thick. Confirms fresh ginger preference.

source_review.text: Great soup! The vegetables caramelize beautifully with this method (though I think it would also work fine in the oven, which would take longer but keep my hands free for other things). I added extra cayenne pepper, lots of black pepper, and used heavy cream instead of 1/2 & 1/2; otherwise followed as written. I didn't find it at all too sweet - these are sweet potatoes, after all. Thanks for the recipe!

modifications_applied.reasoning: Extra spices balance the natural sweetness of sweet potatoes and add depth of flavor.

changes_made.from_text: Salt and freshly ground pepper, to taste
changes_made.to_text: Salt and lots of freshly ground black pepper, to taste

enhancement_summary.expected_impact: Comprehensive enhancements including: convenient canned yams option, roasted vegetables for texture contrast, fresh ginger for better flavor, dairy flexibility for dietary needs, consistency adjustments to prevent thickness, and enhanced spicing for better flavor balance.

description: Made with sweet potatoes and seasoned with ginger, nutmeg, and cayenne pepper, this creamy soup is topped with honey-roasted peanuts.
